title: Glossary of Terms
field_data_points:
Term,Definition 
AMP®  Knowledge Portal,"A general term for referring to the platforms hosting AMP®  project data. Ex. AMP®  Alzheimer's Disease (AD) Knowledge Portal; AMP®  Common Metabolic Diseases (CMD) Knowledge Portal; ARK Portal (AMP®  Autoimmune & Immune-Mediated Diseases (AIM)); AMP®  Parkinson's Disease and Related Disorders (PDRD) Knowledge Portal."
Accelerating Medicines Partnership® (AMP®) program,"Term used to describe the entire AMP program. The AMP program is a public-private partnership between the National Institutes of Health (NIH), the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), multiple biopharmaceutical and life science companies, non-profit and other organizations charged with accelerating new and effective therapies to patients. The program is managed by the Foundation for the National Institutes of Health (FNIH)." 
AMP® project,The FNIH term for individual AMPs. See https://www.nih.gov/amp/amp-projects for the full list.  
BioData Catalyst,"A repository and analysis platform hosted by the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI) of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). AMP® Heart Failure (HF) data are hosted here." 
Centralized data,Data hosted in one repository. 
Clinical data,"Standardized medical information collected from study participants or shared through their health records to characterize their health status. Ex. Demographics, medical history, vital signs, laboratory results, medications, etc."
Controlled (AMP®) data,"Controlled access AMP® data that require researcher and/or research project approval steps such as completion of an institutional data use agreement.  See https://sysbio.hugeamp.org/about.html?page=access for information on available controlled access AMP® data."
Controlled Access Data Repository (CADR),"A data storage and management system that provides restricted access to sensitive, confidential, or proprietary data. This type of repository is designed to ensure that access to the data is granted only to authorized individuals or entities, typically under specific conditions and agreements."
Dataset,"A structured, provenance-tracked collection of information elements with a defined schema and explicit terms of use and stewardship, that supports discovery, access, interpretation, and reuse according to FAIR principles." 
Data interoperability,"A key principle of FAIR. Data interoperability ensures that data is structured in such a way that data from disparate sources can be merged together in a meaningful way."
FAIR principles,"Set of guidelines designed to improve the Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability of digital assets. See https://www.go-fair.org/fair-principles/ for more information."
Federated data model,"A federated data model is a method for organizing and centralizing access to data housed across multiple systems." 
Foundation for the National Institutes of Health (FNIH),"FNIH is a charitable organization established by the US Congress in 1990. Its mission is to 'foster public health through scientific discovery, translational research.'" 
Global Alliance for Genomics and Health (GA4GH),"An international consortium that aims to develop standards for responsibly collecting, storing, analyzing, and sharing genomic data."
Harmonized AMP® data,"Source AMP® data that has been aligned by SysBio into a single, standard format. Ex. Clinical, biospecimen, and assay metadata harmonized to common data elements and mapped to an Extended OMOP Common Data Model; Subset of omics data processed jointly or through functionally equivalent pipelines; Cross-AMP summary stats." 
Metadata,"A set of data that describes and gives information about other data.(i.e., data about data, not participants). Includes descriptive and structural information about files that provides the necessary context to identify, cite, and utilize datasets."
National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) Data Archive,"A repository and analysis platform hosted by the NIHM of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). The AMP® Schizophrenia (SCZ) data are hosted here."
Open (AMP®) Data,"Open access, public data made available through the AMP® projects without use restrictions. See https://sysbio.hugeamp.org/about.html?page=access for information on available open AMP® data." 
Omics assays,"Assays that target biological molecules such as RNA, proteins, metabolites, generally with the gene as a functional unit for analyses. Ex. Genetic assays target DNA, including but not limited to whole genome sequencing, array genotyping, and/or imputation of genotypes." 
Participant level data,"Visualizations or 'row' level data that refer to a single participant."   
Research Auth Service Initiative (RAS),"A systems access service developed by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) that facilitates secure access to NIH’s open and controlled access data repositories." 
Source data,"Originating data. Within SysBio FAIRplex, this refers to the AMP® project data that serves as the foundational 'primary input' for indexing or harmonization."
Summary data,"Descriptive level statistics and visualizations. Data or visuals are aggregated into summary participant counts."
body: Many consortia use shared terminology (e.g., metadata, dataset), but the definitions of these terms can vary. Below are the definitions used by SysBio FAIRplex.
